source_paper_title: On the Smoothed Complexity of Combinatorial Local Search
context:
Page 33, Section 6 (Conclusion and problems)

However, outside of these restricted instances of the problem, the complexity remains unresolved.
title: Smoothed polynomial-time convergence of better-response dynamics in unrestricted congestion games
background_latex:
- A congestion game is defined by a finite player set \(N=[n]\), a finite resource set \(R\), strategy sets \(\Sigma_i\subseteq 2^R\) for each player \(i\in N\), and latency functions \(\kappa_r:[n]\to \mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}\) for each resource \(r\in R\).
- For a strategy profile \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}=(\sigma_1,\dots,\sigma_n)\in \Sigma:=\Sigma_1\times\cdots\times\Sigma_n\), the load on resource \(r\) is \(\ell_r(\boldsymbol{\sigma}) := |\{i\in N: r\in \sigma_i\}|\).
- Player \(i\)'s cost is \(C_i(\boldsymbol{\sigma}) := \sum_{r\in \sigma_i} \kappa_r(\ell_r(\boldsymbol{\sigma}))\).
- A pure Nash equilibrium (PNE) is a profile \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*\in \Sigma\) such that for all players \(i\in N\) and all deviations \(\sigma_i'\in \Sigma_i\), \(C_i(\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*) \le C_i(\sigma_i',\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*_{-i})\).
- Better-response dynamics is the iterative process that, at each step, selects some player \(i\) with a better response \(\sigma_i'\) satisfying \(C_i(\sigma_i',\boldsymbol{\sigma}_{-i}) < C_i(\boldsymbol{\sigma})\) and updates \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}\leftarrow (\sigma_i',\boldsymbol{\sigma}_{-i})\).
- Smoothed analysis model: numerical parameters of the input (e.g., entries defining the latency functions, such as \(\kappa_r(\ell)\) in the explicit model, or coefficients \(\alpha_{r,j}\) in polynomial/step-function models) are drawn independently from continuous distributions supported on \([0,1]\) with densities bounded by \(\phi\).
- The paper proves polynomial smoothed convergence bounds only for restricted families (e.g., \(B\)-restrained congestion games and \((A,B)\)-compact network congestion games).
problem_definition_latex: Determine whether, under the smoothed analysis model with maximum density \(\phi\), there exists an algorithm (in particular, a better-response or best-response dynamics) that computes an exact pure Nash equilibrium \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*\) of an arbitrary (i.e., unrestricted) \(n\)-player congestion game in expected time polynomial in the input size and \(\phi\); or alternatively, prove that such smoothed polynomial-time solvability fails (e.g., by establishing an appropriate hardness or lower bound) for at least one of the standard latency representations (explicit, step-function, or polynomial).
latex:
- A congestion game is defined by a finite player set \(N=[n]\), a finite resource set \(R\), strategy sets \(\Sigma_i\subseteq 2^R\) for each player \(i\in N\), and latency functions \(\kappa_r:[n]\to \mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}\) for each resource \(r\in R\).
- For a strategy profile \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}=(\sigma_1,\dots,\sigma_n)\in \Sigma:=\Sigma_1\times\cdots\times\Sigma_n\), the load on resource \(r\) is \(\ell_r(\boldsymbol{\sigma}) := |\{i\in N: r\in \sigma_i\}|\).
- Player \(i\)'s cost is \(C_i(\boldsymbol{\sigma}) := \sum_{r\in \sigma_i} \kappa_r(\ell_r(\boldsymbol{\sigma}))\).
- A pure Nash equilibrium (PNE) is a profile \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*\in \Sigma\) such that for all players \(i\in N\) and all deviations \(\sigma_i'\in \Sigma_i\), \(C_i(\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*) \le C_i(\sigma_i',\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*_{-i})\).
- Better-response dynamics is the iterative process that, at each step, selects some player \(i\) with a better response \(\sigma_i'\) satisfying \(C_i(\sigma_i',\boldsymbol{\sigma}_{-i}) < C_i(\boldsymbol{\sigma})\) and updates \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}\leftarrow (\sigma_i',\boldsymbol{\sigma}_{-i})\).
- Smoothed analysis model: numerical parameters of the input (e.g., entries defining the latency functions, such as \(\kappa_r(\ell)\) in the explicit model, or coefficients \(\alpha_{r,j}\) in polynomial/step-function models) are drawn independently from continuous distributions supported on \([0,1]\) with densities bounded by \(\phi\).
- The paper proves polynomial smoothed convergence bounds only for restricted families (e.g., \(B\)-restrained congestion games and \((A,B)\)-compact network congestion games).

Determine whether, under the smoothed analysis model with maximum density \(\phi\), there exists an algorithm (in particular, a better-response or best-response dynamics) that computes an exact pure Nash equilibrium \(\boldsymbol{\sigma}^*\) of an arbitrary (i.e., unrestricted) \(n\)-player congestion game in expected time polynomial in the input size and \(\phi\); or alternatively, prove that such smoothed polynomial-time solvability fails (e.g., by establishing an appropriate hardness or lower bound) for at least one of the standard latency representations (explicit, step-function, or polynomial).
open_problem_quote: However, outside of these restricted instances of the problem, the complexity remains unresolved.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
